title: Add --filter to podman system prune
body:
Also document the allowable filters in podman system prune, podman image prune
and podman container prune.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
What about filters on volume pruning, or was that not part of this feature request?

I think volumes have to be handled separately,

podman volume prune itself does not currently support filters.

Another thing we need to add is the ability to prune networks, now that we have network support. but I think those need to be done separatly.  Lets get this one in for now.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
@bblenard I opened issues on volume --filter and on podman network prune, if you want to take a look at those.

> @bblenard I opened issues on volume --filter and on podman network prune, if you want to take a look at those.

I would love to take a swing at it.

@bblenard Thank you so much for your work. :+1: 

@rhatdan How can I test this most easily? So far, I have installed podman using apt on my Ubuntu machine. How would I download and install the master branch?

> @bblenard Thank you so much for your work. 
> 
> @rhatdan How can I test this most easily? So far, I have installed podman using apt on my Ubuntu machine. How would I download and install the master branch?

The way I did it was to follow: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#contributing-to-podman-1 through to the [building binaries](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#building-binaries-and-test-your-changes) section and then using the `make binaries` target. It looks like @rhatdan's changes were merged into master so assuming you have an up to date copy of the repo you should be able to run `bin/podman system prune --filter ...` (after successfully building with `make binaries`) to test. The installation from the ubuntu repos will probably not include these changes so you will have to build the binaries yourself I'm guessing.
Correct you will not see these changes in a distribution (Other then Fedora/Rawhide) until we release podman 3.0.
